title: Wrong inner Join result
description:
### What happens?
Hi, DuckDB Developers!
Please see the below case.  I perform some equivalent join, but I meet some wrong results. 



### To Reproduce

```sql
CREATE TABLE t1(c0 INT);
CREATE TABLE t2(c0 INT);

INSERT INTO t1(c0) VALUES (-18), (NULL);
INSERT INTO t2(c0) VALUES (NULL);




SELECT * FROM t1 INNER JOIN t2 ON ((((t1.c0 NOT BETWEEN t2.c0 AND t2.c0)) IS NULL));
-- Wrong execution result:
-- NULL, NULL
-- NULL, NULL
-- Expected result:
-- -18, NULL
-- NULL, NULL
```


### OS:

MacOS

### DuckDB Version:

v1.3.0-dev2743 0069af20ab

### DuckDB Client:

CLI

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

TheoristCoder

### Affiliation:

Personal Project

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a nightly build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [x] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t3(c0 INT, c1 VARCHAR);
CREATE TABLE t4(c0 INT, c1 FLOAT);
INSERT INTO t4(c0, c1) VALUES (NULL, 3.14), (10, NULL);
SELECT * FROM t3 INNER JOIN t4 ON (t3.c0 IS NULL OR t3.c0 = t4.c0);
key_bug_features: INNER JOIN with NULL handling in condition
